learning_outcomes.description: Identify conservation issues and the key factors involved
Gain perspectives on, both the successes and failures, of national and international conservation initiatives with a focus on frontline conservation regions in Australia and Africa
Produce short and concise summaries, both verbally and in written form, of conservation topics and in depth reviews of the scientific literature relating to particular conservation initiatives
Advance teamwork and group work skills, as well as independent learning skills.
Critically reflect on your development as a professional as a result of your course participation
Advance understanding of applied and novel tools in conservation

assessments.task_description:
This task is linked to the module taught by Professor Salit Kark.

Students will submit a poster (via Turnitin), summarizing their mini-project outcomes, including research goals, hypotheses, methods, results, and discussion, as further explained in Prac sessions and on Blackboard. Only one poster will be submitted per group.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The assessment must be submitted as an electronic version through Turnitin and via Ripple.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
This task is linked to the module taught by Professor Salit Kark

Students will provide one of the others groups feedback on their poster and resources using Ripple. This will be explained in more detail in the practical session and on Blackboard. This is done by each student individually rather than in groups.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.

assessments.assessment_title: Assessment 2 - Conservation genetics analysis and management plan quizzes
assessments.task_description:
This task is linked to the module taught by A/Prof Celine Frere

Assessment 2 - Conservation genomics in class quizzes (during prac time)

Throughout the ‘Omics for Conservation” section (Week 7 to 10), you will be asked to complete three quizzes, 1x in week 8 (10%), 1x in week 9 (10%) and 1x in week 11 (15%). These will focus on the content delivered for that week, including environmental DNA (week 8), microbiomes (week 9), and wildlife disease (week 10). These quizzes are designed to help you develop the skills needed to apply omics approaches to real conservation challenges.

 Each quiz will include:

• Conceptual questions to check your understanding of key ideas

• Data interpretation questions based on the types of analyses explored in workshops

• Applied scenarios where you will use omics tools to address a conservation problem

What you are expected to demonstrate

By completing these quizzes, you should be able to:

• Use core concepts and terminology accurately

• Interpret biological data and identify ecological patterns and limitations

• Apply omics approaches to real-world conservation and management questions

• Clearly explain how data can support decision-making for threatened species conservation

How to approach the quizzes

• Focus on understanding and application, not memorisation

• Be clear, concise, and structured in your answers

• Use examples from lectures, readings, and workshops

• Show your reasoning, especially when proposing management solutions

These quizzes are designed to reflect the kinds of thinking required in conservation science, integrating data, ecology, and decision-making.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT) are emerging tools that may support students in completing this assessment task. Students may appropriately use AI and/or MT in completing this assessment task. Students must clearly reference any use of AI or MT in each instance.

A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Online submission by Turnitin only by the due date and time. No hard copy or assignment cover sheets are required. Submission via email is not accepted.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

End of Semester Exam Period

7/11/2026 - 21/11/2026

This exam requires attendance on campus, or at an approved off-campus exam centre.
assessments.task_description:
The End of Semester exam will consist of short answer questions.

This exam requires attendance on campus, or at an approved off-campus exam centre. You must be able to attend one of these venues. Please refer to the Off-Campus exams information page for further information. Please note a digital exam is not available for this assessment.

Use of Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Translation (MT)

This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to defer this exam.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to defer this exam.
